rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DEAN MARIO TREFAULT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The minor's compromise proceeding ordered funds to be deposited into a blocked account, but the court approved settlement agreement states that Julissa Cabrera's share would be distributed to a trust established for the benefit of Julissa. Petitioner requests that distribution made to Angelica Marquez, as trustee, in a blocked account. 2) Administrator requests $8,410 as extraordinary fees, but only $10,164 in ordinary services has been provided, and statutory fees total $24,321. Objection has been filed. 3) Administrator was reimbursed a total of $33.50 for parking, which are not reimbursable. 4) Attorney requests $43,233 in xo fees. They allege that they have provided ~$55k in ordinary services. Objection has been filed. 5) Original amount anticipated to be paid to minor Julissa Cabrera was $48,519, which has now been reduced to $44,011.99 due to administrative expenses and request for fees. 6) Petitioner requests distribution of Kathie Cordova's share to her attorney, pursuant to order approving settlement agreement. However, Kathie Cordova is now deceased. Petitioner states that the remaining funds after payment of attorney's fees will be paid pursuant to 13101 Affidavit by the attorney. 7) Bank account was allowed to exceed FDIC limit. 8) Petitioner requests after discovered assets be distributed to attorney for Kathie Cordova. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MARGARET E. LOPRESTI 1998 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/19/22. (No earlier availability.) The related case in the petition caption is still the incorrect case number. Household goods were sold on 11/12/20, but none was inventoried. Exhibit B attached to the "supplemental declaration" filed on 8/2/21 was just for the items sold by Maxsold, not those sold by Amazing Adventures or Oakland Silver & Gold. Paragraph 4 of the "supplemental declaration" filed on 8/2/21 does not sufficiently explain why there were "Conserv. Fees Owed to Trust" on 3/19/19. When were those fees ordered in the conservatorship case? And what do fees have to do with advanced costs? Still no date of the order authorizing the $1,019.70 payment to the trustee on 4/30/20 has been set forth. The liabilities schedule attached to the "supplemental declaration" filed on 8/2/21 show the full amount of fees ordered on 12/18/19. (The fiduciary's time sheets have not been fully reviewed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN LAWERENCE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/2021. Same issues. 1) Assignments filed after the petition for final distribution was filed. It appears the petition needs to be amended to change the distribution and to address LRSF 14.36 B. If distribution is to be made to an assignee of an heir or devisee, the assignee must file the original assignment, and the terms thereof must be disclosed in the petition for distribution. Probate Code 11604. [LRSF 14.36 B.] 2) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 3) If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share, or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. [LRSF 14.36 G.] 4) Please explain and file receipts in support of entry 10/12/2020 Reimbursement for Pre-Sale House Expenses of $10,703.89. 5) The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/06/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANNE S. OUTIN REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST UNDER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/19/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The calculation of the bond appears to be incorrect. The recovery on the bond is calculated at 10% of the first $500,000 + 12% of the next $500,000 + 2% of the value over $1,000,000. The remaining escheated assets that were listed on Schedule D-1 of the prior accounting are not reflected in this accounting. (It would have been helpful if the assets listed on Schedule D-1 of the prior accounting had been included as part of Schedule 1 so that Schedule 2 made sense more readily.) All cash exceeding the FDIC insured amount is held at just bank, which does not appear to be prudent. Has Mai Findlay been appointed as the personal representative of Mabel Cocking's estate? Please file evidence of her appointment. Are the successor trustee and her attorney intending to seek fees later? It will be much more challenging for the Court to determine whether fees are reasonable if not sought at the time each accounting is filed, for which accounting period services were provided. Fees granted could be carried forward as liabilities against the estate if the successor trustee and her attorney wished to defer payment of ordered fees. (It is not necessary to recite the I&As in a subsequent accounting, as the assets on hand at the end of the prior accounting determine the starting value of the accounting at hand. There is also no need to attach a copy of a prior petition to a subsequent petition; each petition should stand on its own.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MCNEAL VERNON WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/2021. Objections are in the filing queue but not processed. Issues: 1) Please explain if there has been any update to the claims by SFMTA. 2) Please explain why it is appropriate for the estate to pay SFMTA citations on vehicles then give the vehicles away at a loss to the estate. 3) Attorney please file timesheets for both ordinary and extraordinary services provided to the personal representative so that the Court may determine whether the requested extraordinary fees are "just and reasonable" (Prob. C. 10811(a)). 4) Administrator please file timesheets for both ordinary and extraordinary services so that the Court may determine whether the requested extraordinary fees are "just and reasonable" (Prob. C. 10801). 5) Please explain why the petition alleges certain documents will be filed with the petition and then have not been filed. 6) Please explain why is appropriate for the estate to reimburse what appears to be $1,610.56 for storage costs for property that were worn and of little to no value, donated, and a $250.00 loss to the estate. Unclear why this was rented to allow Kelvin Jones Mackentral Williams to coordinate possession of the property when the petition claims Kelvin Jones is not an heir. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/06/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No inventory and appraisal has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACQUES NOEL JANOT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The decedent executed two wills on the same date. Both wills are identical, except that one of the wills added a provision gifting $2,000,000 to John Scheid. According to the petitioner, the will without the provision for John Scheid was executed after the will including the provision for John Scheid. 2) The petitioner requests fixing a $100,000 bond and opening a blocked account at Charles Schwab to deposit $35,900,000. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.
